title: oxalis conorrhiza
intro: Oxalis conorrhiza, also known as the yellow wood sorrel, is a type of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). It is native to the tropical regions of Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is rich in vitamins and minerals, and its extracts have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Oxalis conorrhiza can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and capsules, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
